author: Senior QA Automation Architect
description: Generates a standard Playwright TypeScript Page Object Model (POM) from a simplified DOM and provided selectors.

input: Simplified DOM (preserving testId, role, label, and placeholder attributes)
BasePage or Shared Components context (if available)
requirements.output: Return ONLY the raw, clean TypeScript source code as Plain Text from the very first line to the last line. Strictly DO NOT wrap the code in markdown code blocks (```), do not include any conversational explanations, notes, or markdown formatting outside the code. Avoid verbose comments; only brief inline technical comments are allowed when necessary for complex locator logic.

architecture: Export ONLY one central Page Object class: export class XxxPage
Allowed to declare supportive TypeScript Interfaces/Types (e.g., FormData) at the top of the file for method parameters.
The constructor must strictly accept: constructor(private readonly page: Page)
The file must be strictly isolated: contain only Locators, navigation methods, UI interaction methods, and Screen State Validations.
Strictly DO NOT include test cases, mock data, fixtures, standalone utility classes, or separate helper classes.
Static/fixed locators must be declared as 'public readonly' properties and fully initialized inside the constructor.
Do not duplicate shared logic if a BasePage or Shared Component is already available in the context.
locator_visibility: All static Locator properties must be declared as 'public readonly' to expose them to the test layer when needed.
Do not use 'private' or 'protected' modifiers for static Locator properties.
Dynamic locator methods that accept arguments (e.g., table rows) are allowed to be 'private' if only used internally within the class.
method_visibility: Internal helper methods (e.g., waiting for loading, resolving rows) must be declared as 'private'.
Atomic Actions and Business Actions must be declared as 'public async'.
Do not use 'protected' modifiers for any methods.
naming_convention: EXCEPTION: Dynamic elements that accept parameters must not be declared as static properties; they must use business methods prefixed with 'get', such as getXxxRow(param), getXxxOption(param), getXxxItem(param).
locator_strategy: Strict locator priority order: getByTestId → getByRole → getByLabel → getByPlaceholder → semantic CSS → provided selector.
If a semantic locator is available, always prioritize it over others.
Do not select a locator simply because it is shorter or because it matches the provided selector list.
Only use the provided selector when no suitable testId, role, label, or placeholder exists in the DOM.
When multiple valid locators are available, select the most stable one following the defined priority.
Strictly FORBIDDEN: Absolute XPath, layout-dependent CSS selectors (e.g., long div > div chains), and dynamic index methods (.nth(), .first(), .last()) unless no other stable alternative exists.
Discard locators based on placeholders or text that are prone to changing if a more stable semantic alternative is available.
action_methods: Prioritize a clean separation between Atomic Actions and Business Actions.
Atomic Actions should only be created when the element is highly reusable, has specific internal logic, or requires individual validation.
Business Actions must reuse existing Atomic Actions rather than rewriting raw UI interactions.
Do not duplicate UI interaction logic across multiple methods.
All input data must be passed via method parameters; hard-coding test data is strictly forbidden.
form_strategy: If the form contains fewer than 5 fields, primitive parameters are acceptable.
If the form contains 5 or more fields, you must use an object parameter (e.g., data: ClientFormData) and define the corresponding interface at the top of the file.
waiting_strategy: Leverage Playwright's default Auto-wait mechanism. Do not add redundant expect(locator).toBeVisible() statements before click() or fill() operations.
Only introduce extra expect or wait conditions when handling asynchronous components (e.g., Modals, Dialogs, dynamic Dropdowns/Grids, Toasts, Spinners).

If a Spinner or Loading Overlay is present in the DOM, you must implement a private method: private async waitForLoadingCompleted() using expect(spinner).toBeHidden(), and call it after actions that trigger data loading.
If a Toast or Message is present, implement a suitable helper to wait for its appearance/disappearance before proceeding.
Strictly FORBIDDEN: waitForTimeout(), sleep(), or any hard-coded delays.
complex_structures: Navigation must use relative paths (e.g., /login); absolute URLs are strictly forbidden.
Modals/Dialogs must have isolated locators and dedicated action methods (e.g., open, close, confirm).
Tables/Grids must implement the Row-Key Pattern using a helper method: private getRowByKey(key: string): Locator.
All interactions inside a row (such as Edit, Delete, View buttons) must be scoped from that Row Locator; row index must not be used.
Business Actions interacting with tables/grids must reuse the getRowByKey() method.
component_reuse: If the input indicates the presence of Shared Components, prioritize reusing them over generating new locators.
Do not duplicate locators for common elements like Left Menu, Header, Footer, Pagination, Search Filters, or global Toasts.
If a component already exists in the framework, inject or utilize it through the constructor.
framework_awareness: If a BasePage is provided, prioritize extending it (e.g., export class XxxPage extends BasePage).
If the BasePage already contains helper methods for loading or navigation, do not redefine or duplicate them.
If a Shared Component is already present, do not regenerate equivalent locators or helpers.
assertions: Only allow assertions that serve Screen State Validation (e.g., toBeVisible, toBeHidden, toHaveText) to support the next step in the flow.
Strictly FORBIDDEN: Checking test case business logic or final data outcomes (Business Assertions) inside the Page Object class.
imports: Standard structure: import { Page, Locator, expect } from '@playwright/test';
Only import modules that are actively declared and used in the code; redundant imports are strictly forbidden.
Do not import Browser, BrowserContext, chromium, firefox, webkit, or test if they are not used. Do not import directly from playwright-core.

workflow.step_1: Analyze the comprehensive DOM structure, provided selectors, and framework/BasePage context.
workflow.step_2: Declare the Import block and any supporting Interfaces/Types for Form Objects if required.
workflow.step_3: Define the Class structure (extending BasePage if requested) and initialize all public readonly Locator properties inside the Constructor.
workflow.step_4: Build private helper methods for asynchronous UI handling (waitForLoadingCompleted) and Table scoping (getRowByKey).
workflow.step_5: Write the Atomic Actions layer for reusable individual UI components.
workflow.step_6: Finalize the Business Actions layer by chaining Atomic Actions and integrating corresponding asynchronous wait mechanisms.
